entry.title: S/4HANA 2022 SP02: DE_CII/ZUGFeRD fails with “UBL_MAP_SOURCE” mapping error
entry.content:
Hello,

we are implementing outbound German e-invoices in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud 2022 SP02.

Our current setup already generates and displays XRechnung 3.0 in UBL format successfully. We are now trying to generate the CII-based format for ZUGFeRD.

Configuration:
- SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud 2022 SP02
- Country: Germany
- Source document: SD billing document
- Billing type: F2
- eDocument type: DE_CII
- AIF namespace: /EDCII
- AIF interface: CREATEINV, version 1
- SAP data structure: EDOC_CII_MAPPING_SOURCE
- Init function before mapping: EDOC_AIF_INIT_REQUEST_MAPPING

When selecting “Display XML” for the DE_CII eDocument in EDOC_COCKPIT, we receive the following error:

“Field UBL_MAP_SOURCE in component EDOC_CII_MAPPING_SOURCE not found”
The original message in: "&Feld &UBL_MAP_SOURCE& in Komponente &EDOC_CII_MAP&ING_SOURCE& nicht vorhanden&"

During debugging, the field UBL_MAP_SOURCE is actually present at runtime and has the type EDOC_UBL_MAPPING_SOURCE.

However, the following fields are initial in EDOC_CII_MAPPING_SOURCE:

- UBL_MAPPING_CLASS
- UBL_MAPPING_METHOD
- UBL_INTERFACE_ID
- UBL_EDOC_TYPE
- UBL_REQ_TARGET_STRUCTURE_NAME
- UBL_REQ_TARGET_FIELD_NAME

The error occurs in:

CL_EDOC_MAP_CII=>MAP_CREATEINVI
→ REUSE_UBL_MAPPING

The method REUSE_UBL_MAPPING expects the fields listed above to be populated. The method CL_EDOCUMENT_EU_EMAIL=>GET_UBL_MAPPING_INFO returns immediately because MV_UBL_EDOC_CLASS is initial.

We also checked CL_EDOC_MAP_CII=>GET_MAPPING_SOURCE_DATA. This method contains the logic to determine the UBL mapping class and the /EDUBL AIF target structure, but it is not called in the XML display execution path we traced.

SAP Notes checked so far:
- 2947961: not relevant in our system
- 3740587: not implemented
- 3708482: currently under review

Questions:

1. What is the correct standard configuration for DE_CII/ZUGFeRD in S/4HANA 2022 SP02?
2. Which SAP Note or Support Package provides the missing UBL mapping initialization?
3. Is SAP Note 3708482 relevant for this specific issue?
4. Should DE_CII reuse the UBL mapping from the existing DE_INV/XRechnung configuration, and if so, where is this connection configured?

Any advice or a reference implementation would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Internal & External Procurement in asset management
entry.content:
Greetings,

immediate purchase requisition if stock is unavailable is not possible in standard. As you observed yourself you need to run MRP to generate procurement proposals. I guess you could develop some enhancements to run MRP after order is saved but this purchase requisition would not be linked to the order. You would need to be careful here to limit the MRP run only to specific materials.

The mandatory fields when selecting item category N can be defaulted. This is useful because usually employees working with maintenance order to not know or do not need to know the details. The following details can be defaulted:

  • Cost Element
  • Purchasing Organization
  • Purchasing Group
  • Material Group

Create the profile:

SPRO -> Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Maintenance and Service Processing -> Maintenance and Service Orders -> Functions and Settings for Order Types -> Procurement -> Create Default Value Profiles for External Procurement

Assign profile to maintenance order/plant combination:

SPRO -> Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Maintenance and Service Processing -> Maintenance and Service Orders -> Functions and Settings for Order Types -> Default Values for Task List Data and Profile Assignments

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Unable to Modify G/L Account During Goods Receipt Posting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Subject: Unable to Modify G/L Account During Goods Receipt Posting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Hi Experts,

We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have encountered a scenario where we are unable to modify the G/L Account during Goods Receipt (Movement Type 101) against a Purchase Order.

While posting the Goods Receipt, the G/L Account field under the Account Assignment tab is displayed as read-only and cannot be edited.

Business Scenario

In our business, there are situations where an incorrect G/L Account is assigned during Purchase Requisition (PR) or Purchase Order (PO) creation. Before posting the Goods Receipt, the business would like to correct the G/L Account so that the accounting entry is posted to the appropriate General Ledger account.

However, the system does not allow any modification to the G/L Account during GR posting.

Questions

1. Is this the standard behavior in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

2. Is there any configuration (SSCUI), business catalog/role, or extensibility option that allows the G/L Account field to be editable during Goods Receipt?

3. If this is standard system behavior, what is the recommended SAP best practice to handle cases where an incorrect G/L Account has been assigned in the PR or PO?

4. Is the only supported approach to update the PO (if possible) or reverse and recreate the procurement documents before posting the Goods Receipt?

Business Impact

The inability to modify the G/L Account during Goods Receipt prevents users from correcting an incorrect G/L Account assigned during PR or PO creation. As a result, Goods Receipts may be posted to incorrect General Ledger accounts, requiring document reversals, manual accounting adjustments, or recreation of procurement documents. This increases operational effort, delays processing, and may impact the accuracy of financial postings.

Any guidance or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Groovy in your SAP Cloud Integration iFlows is application code. Here is what to check.
entry.content:
This is a very timely and practical post. Groovy scripting in SAP Cloud Integration is often misunderstood as just a ‘developer’s add‑on,’ but your explanation makes it clear that it is application code that directly shapes integration logic. The examples you’ve shared highlight how Groovy can extend iFlow capabilities beyond configuration, enabling dynamic payload handling, custom validations, and enriched transformations.

From an enterprise architecture perspective, the key takeaway is that Groovy should be treated with the same governance and lifecycle discipline as any other application code — versioning, testing, and documentation are critical. This ensures that integrations remain maintainable and compliant as they scale.

entry.title: Re: How are you implementing AI-powered search across SAP business documents?
entry.content:
In production landscapes, we’ve seen three main approaches to AI‑powered search across SAP business documents:

  • Joule + Document Grounding: Joule provides natural language understanding while Document Grounding ensures queries are resolved against SAP’s system of record. This is the most ‘native’ approach and keeps authorizations intact.

  • Custom RAG Pipelines: Some teams build retrieval‑augmented generation pipelines where SAP documents are indexed in a vector store (e.g., HANA Cloud or Datasphere embeddings). Queries are enriched with metadata like company code, contract type, or payment terms before being passed to an LLM.

  • Secure Gateway to External LLMs: Others expose SAP documents to external models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Azure OpenAI) via a controlled gateway. The critical piece here is enforcing SAP authorizations — typically by filtering search results at the gateway before they reach the LLM.

Key lessons learned:

  • Always treat SAP as the system of record; AI should augment search, not bypass governance.

  • Authorization enforcement is non‑negotiable — whether you use Joule or external models, users must only see documents they are entitled to.

  • Metadata enrichment (e.g., payment terms, partner IDs, document type) dramatically improves search accuracy.

  • Keep the architecture modular so you can swap AI providers without re‑engineering the SAP integration.

entry.title: Re: SAP Automation via APIs
entry.content:
Hello @MariaCristina_Forero 

Thank you for your question

 

Public cloud supports integration through released APIs and communication scenarios. The exact APIs currently enabled in your system cannot be confirmed without reviewing its communication configuration.

Here are the available APIs:

You can use Display Communication Scenario in the system to review all available scenarios, their scope dependencies, inbound and outbound services, supported authentication methods, and existing arrangements. Ensure you use Communication Arrangement to identify which scenarios and APIs are ready.

See the following references for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/baa798b6a1024d229ca3f51bde6f24f9.html?locale=en-US

For the complete public catalog, you can use Business Accelerator Hub and filter by the public cloud package. The hub provides API documentation, entities, payload examples, communication scenario IDs, and test capabilities.

See the following reference for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/1e60f14bdc224c2c975c8fa8bcfd7f3f.html?locale=en-US

Here are integration services:

Public cloud provides released OData and SOAP APIs. Availability depends on the activated business scope and the communication scenario delivered for the API.

See the following reference for more insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/1e60f14bdc224c2c975c8fa8bcfd7f3f.html?locale=en-US

Here is the preferred procedure:

  • You should find the required API and Business Accelerator Hub.
  • Ensure you check its communication scenario, direction, operations, and authentication.
  • In the system, you can create a communication user where required.
  • It is important you create a communication system that represents your external application.
  • You can create a Communication Arrangement for the relevant SAP_COM_xxxx scenario
  • Ensure you use the exact service URL displayed under Inbound Service in that arrangement.

See the following reference for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/03c04db2a7434731b7fe21dca77440da/a5550ea977b24a6eb6ce1ce832088567.html?locale=en-US

 

Automating Flori screens for long term integrations are not preferred when a released API already exist.

Here are the authentication and access:

Authentication is defined per communication scenario, so not every method is available for every API. Depending on the scenario, supported methods can include:

  • Basic authentication with a communication user.
  • X.509 certificate authentication
  • OAuth 2.0
  • OAuth based principal propagation for supported BTP scenarios

OAuth client credentials is an OAuth flow using a client ID and secret. It is not automatically available for every service. Certificate based authentication are preferred when the scenario supports it.

Key restrictions are that only released APIs may be used, the required scope must be active, the communication arrangement grants only for the scenario predefined authorizations, and technical users must not be treated as unrestricted business users.

 

Let me know if you have any further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Connecting SAP PaPM Cloud (Universal Model) to Google BigQuery via SAP HANA Cloud Virtual Tables.
entry.content:
Architecture Overview

The data flow follows a clean 3-tier sequence:

  • Google BigQuery: Holds the raw transactional data (e.g., ticket extracts, gross amounts, payment methods).
  • SAP HANA Cloud (SDA / Remote Source): Connects to BigQuery via the bigqueryrest adapter and exposes the tables as Virtual Tables inside a designated schema (e.g., SAP_PAPM_ADMIN).
  • SAP PaPM Cloud (Universal Model): Consumes the Virtual Table through a HANA Schema connection to map fields into the modeling environment.

Key Setup Steps & Lessons Learned

1. SSL Certificates in SAP HANA Cloud

Before establishing the BigQuery Remote Source, ensure that the required Google Trust Services certificates are imported into the SAP HANA Cloud Certificate Store and properly bound to the SDA PSE.

Tip: If you encounter SSL handshake issues during connection tests, verify that the GTS Root R4 certificate is properly imported and bound to _SAP_DB_ACCESS_PSE_SDA.

2. Creating the Remote Source and Virtual Table

Using SAP HANA Database Explorer, we configured the Remote Source pointing to the BigQuery project and dataset. Then, we created the target Virtual Table inside the SAP_PAPM_ADMIN schema.

Figure 1 - BigQuery Remote Source, Virtual Table setup in SAP HANA Explorer, and HANA Schema connection configuration in PaPM Cloud.

3. Handling the PaPM Cloud UI Metadata Validation (^.{0,200}$)

When synchronizing the HANA Schema connection in PaPM Cloud, you might encounter a string length format validation error similar to:

Value 'Specifies whether the cached is enabled for the view or not: ...'
is not in specified format '^.{0,200}$'
Column: Description

 

Why does this happen? When HANA Cloud creates a Virtual Table over BigQuery, it inherits extended metadata comments into the table/view description. PaPM Cloud's UI enforces a strict 200-character limit on description fields during model synchronization.

The Solutions

We implemented two complementary solutions to bypass and fix this issue completely:

  • Option A: Apply a Model Filter in PaPM Connection (Recommended): In the General Information tab of your PaPM HANA Schema connection, use the Model Filter field to specify only your target table (e.g., XXXXXXX_extractos_transacciones). This automatically excludes system views or unrelated schema objects with long descriptions.

Figure 2 - Using the Model Filter setting in PaPM Cloud Connection to isolate the target Virtual Table and avoid metadata issues.

  • Option B: Overwrite the HANA Table Comment via SQL: Run a short SQL statement in SAP HANA Database Explorer to replace the inherited metadata comment with a concise string:

COMMENT ON TABLE "SAP_PAPM_ADMIN"."IXXXXXXX_extractos_transacciones"
IS 'Transacciones BigQuery';

Figure 3 - Overwriting the inherited metadata comment using SQL in SAP HANA Database Explorer.

Result: Consuming Data in PaPM Universal Model

Once the description comment was updated in HANA and the Model Filter was applied in PaPM Cloud, the model synchronization completed seamlessly. We mapped the BigQuery fields to our PaPM Environment dimensions, and running a "Show Data" action immediately displayed live BigQuery records inside the SAP PaPM Cloud UI!

Figure 4 - Virtual Table functions and field mappings active inside the PaPM Universal Model environment.

Figure 5 - Live BigQuery records successfully displayed using the Show Data action inside SAP PaPM Cloud UI.

Bonus: Direct BigQuery SQL Query

For verification, you can also query the source dataset directly in Google BigQuery:

SELECT *
FROM `gen-lang-client-003xfdsf.xxxxxxxx_es_jesap.extractos_transacciones`
LIMIT 100;

Figure 6 - Source transactional dataset and SQL query execution in Google BigQuery console.

Conclusion

Connecting BigQuery to SAP PaPM Cloud via HANA Virtual Tables provides a powerful, real-time data flow without double-storing data. The two key takeaways from our experience:

  • Always ensure SSL certificates (GTS Root R4) are properly aligned in SAP HANA Cloud Certificate Store.
  • Keep table descriptions concise and leverage the Model Filter in PaPM Cloud connections to prevent UI metadata validation errors.

Have you tried connecting external cloud databases to SAP PaPM Cloud? Share your thoughts and questions in the comments below!

entry.title: Re: Set BUT000-XBLCK = X; lfa1 - sperr = 'X' and lfb1-zah...
entry.content:
CVP_XBLCK and not XBLCK ?

This code updates BUT000-XBLCK, LFA1-SPERR, LFB1-ZAHLS (and also BUT000-XDELE, LFA1-SPERM, LFA1-LOEVM, LFB1-SPERR, LFB1-LOEVM):

cl_md_bp_maintain=>maintain( 
EXPORTING i_test_run = abap_false
          i_data     = VALUE cvis_ei_extern_t(
(
partner  = VALUE bus_ei_extern(
           header       = VALUE bus_ei_header( 
                          object_instance = VALUE bus_ei_instance(
                                            bpartner     = is_partner-partner
                                            bpartnerguid = is_partner-partner_guid )
                          object_task     = 'U' )
           central_data = VALUE bus_ei_central_data(
                          common = VALUE bus_ei_bupa_central(
                                   data  = VALUE bus_ei_bupa_central_data(
                                           bp_centraldata = VALUE bus_ei_struc_central(
                                                            centralarchivingflag = ls_but000->xdele
                                                            centralblock         = ls_but000->xblck ) )
                                   datax = VALUE bus_ei_bupa_central_data_xflag(
                                           bp_centraldata = VALUE bus_ei_struc_central_x(
                                                            centralarchivingflag = 'X'
                                                            centralblock         = 'X' ) ) ) ) ) )
vendor   = VALUE vmds_ei_extern(
           header          = VALUE #(
                             object_instance = VALUE vmds_instance( 
                                               lifnr = is_partner-partner )
                             object_task     = 'U' )
           central_data    = VALUE vmds_ei_central_data(
                             central = VALUE vmds_ei_vmd_central(
                                       data  = VALUE vmds_ei_vmd_central_data(
                                               loevm = ls_lfa1->loevm
                                               sperr = ls_lfa1->sperr
                                               sperm = ls_lfa1->sperm )
                                       datax = VALUE vmds_ei_vmd_central_data_xflag(
                                               loevm = 'X'
                                               sperr = 'X'
                                               sperm = 'X' ) ) ) )
           company_data    = VALUE vmds_ei_vmd_company(
                             current_state = ''
                             company       = VALUE vmds_ei_company_t(
                                             ( task     = 'U'
                                               data_key = VALUE vmds_ei_company_key(
                                                          bukrs = <ls_lfb1>-bukrs )
                                               data     = VALUE vmds_ei_company_data(
                                                          zahls = <ls_lfb1>-zahls
                                                          sperr = <ls_lfb1>-sperr
                                                          loevm = <ls_lfb1>-loevm )
                                               datax    = VALUE vmds_ei_company_datax(
                                                          zahls = 'X'
                                                          sperr = 'X'
                                                          loevm = 'X' ) ) ) )
) )
IMPORTING e_return   = DATA(lt_return_md_bp) ).

Sandra

entry.title: Restricting Journal Entry Reversals
entry.content:
A common requirement is to allow users to display, edit, or post journal entries while preventing them from reversing journal entries.

With the introduction of App Authorization Variants (AAVs), this can now be achieved through standard role configuration without using UI Adaptation.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Restricting Journal Entry Reversals
entry.content:
The Manage Journal Entries app (F0717A) separates the reversal function into its own App Authorization Variant.

The relevant variants are:

  • F0717A_TRAN – Display and Edit Journal Entries
  • F0717A_85_TRAN – Reverse Journal Entries
  • F8214_TRAN – Schedule Mass Reverse Jobs

The reversal function is only available when F0717A_85_TRAN is assigned to the user's business role.

To restrict reversals:

  1. Open the Maintain Business Roles app.
  2. Open the relevant business role.
  3. Navigate to the IAM Apps section.
  4. Search for F0717A_85_TRAN.
  5. Deactivate or remove the App Authorization Variant.
  6. Save the business role.

After the role changes take effect, users will continue to have the functionality granted by the remaining App Authorization Variants, while the Reverse Journal Entries action will no longer be available.

If users should also be prevented from performing mass reversals, ensure that F8214_TRAN is also not assigned.

This provides a standard authorization-based approach for controlling journal entry reversal permissions at a more granular level.

entry.title: Joule with SAP Cloud ALM available to all SAP Cloud ALM customers.
entry.content:
Agentic AI is coming to SAP Cloud ALM — and it starts here.

We are extremely excited to announce the availability of Joule with SAP Cloud ALM for all eligible SAP Cloud ALM Customers, the first step in bringing agentic AI capabilities directly into the SAP Cloud ALM experience. This initial release delivers a conversational AI assistant embedded within SAP Cloud ALM, helping users navigate the application and find the information they need — faster and more intuitively.

What is Joule with SAP Cloud ALM?

Joule with SAP Cloud ALM brings the power of SAP’s AI assistant, Joule, into the heart of your application lifecycle management platform. Just as Joule has already transformed how users interact with other SAP solutions, it now extends that same intelligent, context-aware experience to SAP Cloud ALM.

In this initial release, Joule with SAP Cloud ALM focuses on two core capabilities:

  • Navigation assistance — Get to where you need to go within SAP Cloud ALM quickly. Instead of navigating through menus, simply ask Joule. Whether you are looking for a specific monitoring view, a project task, or an operations dashboard, Joule guides you there directly.
  • Information and guidance — Ask questions about SAP Cloud ALM features, functions, and processes and receive contextual answers. No more hunting through documentation — Joule brings the answers to you.

These capabilities are designed to reduce friction for both new users getting familiar with SAP Cloud ALM and experienced users who want to move faster in their day-to-day work.

Built on a Foundation for the Future

This release marks the beginning, not the end. Joule with SAP Cloud ALM is architected as an extensible AI assistant, if the capabilities initially delivered might seem limited, Joule with SAP Cloud ALM is built to be enriched by specialized agents over time.

SAP Cloud ALM already covers the full application lifecycle — from implementation planning and process design, through testing and deployment, to operations monitoring and continuous improvement, as well as the support to migrate from ECC to SAP S/4 HANA. Each of these domains is a natural candidate for deeper AI assistance. As Joule with SAP Cloud ALM evolves, additional agents will be introduced to support use cases such as (just examples):

  • Dashboard Generation
  • Project setup and onboarding
  • Test case generation
  • Operational intelligence and anomaly detection
  • Migration and modernization agents and assistants.

This achievement officially opens the agentic era of SAP Cloud ALM with a clear vision: Autonomous ALM and the Autonomous Enterprise. With a Joule-orchestrated, agent-powered ALM platform that keeps human decisions at the center application lifecycle management is about to change dramatically.

How to Get Started

Activating Joule with SAP Cloud ALM is straightforward. The feature is available via SAP for Me (me.sap.com), SAP’s central customer portal.

To activate it:

  • Navigate to SAP for Me (me.sap.com).
  • Go to the "Portfolio & Products" section, then to the "Business AI" tab.
  • Direct Link: https://me.sap.com/portfolio/businessAI 
  • Locate the feature "Joule with SAP Cloud ALM".
  • Follow the activation steps to enable the feature for your tenant.

Note: You will need the appropriate administrative permissions in SAP for Me to activate features for your organization.

Once activated, Joule will be available directly within your SAP Cloud ALM tenant, ready to assist your teams immediately.

Commercial Aspect

It is important to know that Joule with SAP Cloud ALM is available without additional costs, meaning that it will not consume AI Units you will still need to accept the AI general terms and conditions before being able to activate it. This is the case when you have procured AI Units already.
However the agents that will be added to Joule with SAP Cloud ALM will be subject to their own commercial models and may consume AI Units. These agents will also need to be activated in SAP for me.

Why This Matters

SAP Cloud ALM has established itself as the cloud-native ALM platform for SAP customers — the strategic hub for managing implementations, operations, ECC migrations and continuous innovation across modern SAP landscapes. Embedding Joule into this platform reflects SAP’s commitment to making AI a native part of how customers manage their SAP solutions, not an add-on to be integrated separately.

The introduction of Joule with SAP Cloud ALM is part of SAP’s broader Business AI strategy, which positions Joule as the common engagement layer across the SAP portfolio. Every interaction you have with Joule within SAP Cloud ALM is grounded in the context of your ALM data and processes — making the assistance relevant, not generic.

Looking Ahead

The journey with Joule with SAP Cloud ALM is just beginning. We are committed to continuously expanding its capabilities and deepening its integration with SAP Cloud ALM’s rich feature set. Expect to see new agent-powered capabilities introduced in upcoming releases, bringing AI assistance to increasingly complex lifecycle management scenarios.

At the moment Joule with SAP Cloud ALM is restricted to SAP Cloud ALM agents and cannot interact with other LoB agent. This will be addressed in the future.

Stay tuned to the SAP Community and the SAP Cloud ALM release notes for updates on upcoming capabilities, innovations and agents.

We look forward to hearing your feedback as you explore Joule with SAP Cloud ALM. Together, we are building the future of autonomous application lifecycle management.

Enjoy!!!

entry.title: The packaging blind spot in PLM: Why your product lives in SAP, but its packaging doesn’t
entry.content:
Imagine this: A new product is ready for launch. The formulation has been approved. Or the BOM is complete. Production is prepared. Supply chain is aligned.
Then someone asks a seemingly simple question: “Which packaging version is actually going to market?” Suddenly, the room gets quiet.

The packaging specification sits in a separate system. The artwork is managed by an external agency. Approval records are buried in email threads. Regulatory information lives in spreadsheets. Sustainability data is being collected manually for reporting.

Sound familiar?

The irony is that this scenario doesn't just happen in a few isolated organizations. It happens across industries: from consumer goods and food & beverage to chemicals, life sciences, and manufacturing.

And now, the upcoming EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation (PPWR) is bringing this issue into sharp focus. Designed to increase transparency, sustainability, recyclability, and compliance across the entire packaging lifecycle, PPWR requires companies to know far more about their packaging than many do today.
As a result, many organizations are realizing something important:

Packaging is no longer just packaging.

It is a product component, a compliance object, a sustainability data source, and a critical part of the customer experience – all at the same time.

SAP is often not the problem.

Most SAP-centric companies have spent years building a robust enterprise data foundation. Materials are managed in SAP. Bills of material are managed in SAP. Engineering documents are managed in SAP. Change processes are managed in SAP.

Yet surprisingly, packaging and artwork often live somewhere else.

Packaging specifications are stored in specialized systems. Artwork files reside on shared drives or agency platforms. Approval workflows happen through emails. Data for sustainability reporting is collected manually from multiple sources.

For years, this approach was manageable. Today, it is becoming increasingly difficult.

The drivers are familiar:

  • PPWR compliance requirements
  • Sustainability and circular economy initiatives
  • Country-specific labeling regulations
  • Growing product and packaging variants
  • Increasing collaboration with external agencies and suppliers

As complexity grows, disconnected information quickly becomes a business risk.

The question nobody asks

In many customer discussions, we hear the same statement: “We manage packaging outside of SAP.” The more interesting question is: Why? After all, packaging is inseparable from the product itself.

To understand a product, companies increasingly need visibility into:

  • packaging structures
  • packaging materials
  • recycled content
  • sustainability attributes
  • labeling requirements
  • approved artwork versions
  • regulatory documentation

Without the right context, all of these elements become disconnected data points.

What organizations really need is a complete picture of the product and everything that belongs to it.

Packaging is becoming a digital product

This is where SAP Engineering Control Center | SAP ECTR enters the conversation. Many SAP professionals know SAP ECTR as a platform for document management and engineering integration. What is often overlooked is its ability to connect packaging-related information directly to the SAP product context.

Instead of managing information across multiple silos, organizations can establish a unified view of:

  • product data
  • packaging structures
  • specifications
  • material information
  • technical documentation
  • artwork files
  • approval status

The result is a shared information space where packaging development, marketing, quality management, regulatory affairs, and external partners can work with the same data foundation.

And that is where the real value emerges. Because the goal is not simply to store documents in SAP. 

The goal is to make packaging part of the digital product definition.

PPWR is really a data challenge

Most discussions around PPWR focus on recyclability targets, reporting obligations, and material requirements. But beneath the regulatory requirements lies a much broader challenge:

Data transparency.

Most companies already possess the information they need. The problem is that the information is scattered across different systems, departments, partners, and file formats.

When sustainability teams need material insights, when regulatory teams need compliance evidence, or when packaging engineers need the latest approved artwork, the effort often turns into a data scavenger hunt.

Creating a single source of truth for product and packaging information is therefore not just an IT initiative.

It is becoming a business necessity.

 There is no one-size-fits-all approach

At DSC Software AG, we have learned that successful Packaging & Artwork Management initiatives rarely start with technology.

They start with understanding the company's reality.

Questions such as these define the right path forward:

  • Which SAP systems are already in place?
  • Where are packaging specifications managed today?
  • How extensive is collaboration with agencies, suppliers, and co-packers?
  • Which regulatory requirements must be supported?
  • Does the organization prefer an on-premises, cloud, or hybrid strategy?

Only after understanding the existing landscape can companies design an architecture that truly fits their business.

For some organizations, that may mean expanding existing SAP capabilities. For others, it may involve introducing new packaging and artwork processes while preserving established workflows.

The common denominator remains the same:

Product, packaging, artwork, and compliance data belong together.

And the closer these elements are connected, the easier it becomes to achieve transparency, consistency, and regulatory readiness.

Final Thought

Perhaps the biggest misconception in the industry is that packaging is still considered an extension of the product. In reality, packaging has become part of the product itself.

As PPWR and sustainability requirements continue to reshape the market, organizations need more than compliant packaging. They need connected packaging data.

So here is a simple question for every SAP professional:

If your product already lives in SAP, why does its packaging still live somewhere else?

If you want to dive deeper into the topic, visit our website.

entry.title: Uploading File into SAP DMS Using ABAP
entry.content:
Introduction

In many SAP applications, users upload supporting documents through a Fiori application or an OData service. These documents often need to be stored securely in SAP Document Management System (DMS) and linked to a business object such as an employee master record.

This blog explains how to upload a PDF received through an OData service into SAP DMS using BAPI_DOCUMENT_CREATE2. The implementation also links the document to the employee master (PA0001) and removes the temporary file after a successful upload.

Read the Temporary Directory

The temporary directory is retrieved from the USER_DIR table using a configured directory alias. This directory acts as a staging location where the document is temporarily written before being uploaded to SAP DMS. Replace the directory alias with the one configured in your system.

SELECT SINGLE * FROM user_dir INTO ls_user_dir WHERE aliass = '<your directory>'.

Generate a Unique File Name

A unique filename is generated by combining business-specific information such as the employee number, process reference, and current date. This helps prevent duplicate filenames and makes document identification easier.

CONCATENATE '<NAME>' lv_pernr process_ref sy-datum '.pdf' INTO ev_filename.

Write the File to the Application Server

The PDF content is written to the temporary directory on the application server in binary mode. This physical file is later used by SAP DMS during document creation.

OPEN DATASET lv_filedir FOR OUTPUT IN BINARY MODE.
LOOP AT it_content INTO ls_tab.
 TRANSFER ls_tab TO lv_filedir. 
ENDLOOP. 
CLOSE DATASET lv_filedir.

Prepare the File Details

The file information is populated in the BAPI_DOC_FILES2 structure, including the storage category, file path, filename, and workstation application. Replace the storage category with the one configured in your SAP DMS environment.

ls_files-originaltype = '1'. 
ls_files-storagecategory = '<your storage category>'. 
ls_files-docpath = ls_user_dir-dirname.
ls_files-docfile = ev_filename. 
ls_files-wsapplication = 'PDF'.

Prepare the Document Header

The document header is maintained using the BAPI_DOC_DRAW2 structure. Specify the appropriate document type configured in your system and provide a meaningful description for the document.

 

ls_documentdata-documenttype = '<document type>'.
CONCATENATE '<NAME>' lv_pernr process_ref sy-datum INTO ls_documentdata-description SEPARATED BY space. 
ls_documentdata-username = sy-uname.

Create the Object Link

The object link associates the DMS document with a business object in SAP. Replace the object type and object key with the values relevant to your business scenario.

ls_objectlink-objecttype = 'PA0001'. 
ls_objectlink-objectkey = pernr. 
APPEND ls_objectlink TO lt_objectlinks.

Create the Document in SAP DMS

Call BAPI_DOCUMENT_CREATE2 to create the document in SAP DMS. The BAPI uses the document header, file details, and object link information to create the document and establish the relationship with the business object.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_DOCUMENT_CREATE2' 
EXPORTING 
documentdata = ls_documentdata 
pf_ftp_dest = 'SAPFTPA' 
pf_http_dest = 'SAPHTTPA' 
IMPORTING 
documenttype = lv_docty 
documentnumber = lv_docnr 
documentpart = lv_docpr 
documentversion = lv_docvr 
return = ls_ret 
TABLES 
documentfiles = lt_files 
objectlinks = lt_objectlinks.

Commit the Transaction

After the document is created successfully, commit the transaction to permanently save the document in SAP DMS.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT' 
EXPORTING 
wait = abap_true.

Delete the Temporary File

Once the upload is complete, delete the temporary file from the application server to free up storage space and keep the temporary directory clean.

DELETE DATASET lv_filedir.

Conclusion

Using BAPI_DOCUMENT_CREATE2 provides a standard and reliable approach for storing documents in SAP DMS. By combining document metadata, physical file information, and object links, uploaded files from an OData service can be securely stored and associated with employee master data. Cleaning up temporary files after the upload completes ensures efficient use of application server storage and follows SAP best practices.

entry.title: kob1 by the variant selection orders no actual line items were selected-KB420
entry.content: We use transaction kob1- Orders: Actual Line Items or S_ALR_87012993- Orders: Actual/Plan/Variance. When the selection variant orders is filled in selection parameter 'Order Group'  report returns error message KB420 "No actual line items were selected".  However there are actual line items by the orders which are contented in this order group when they  are filled in selection parameter Order. There are no issues with authorization.

entry.title: Re: Unable to get ISBP number in DMEE file
entry.content:
Hi, Amaresh.

Thank you for reaching out.

To better understand your requirement and provide accurate guidance, could you please clarify the following points:

Payment type: Which payment method are you processing — TED, DOC, PIX, or another type? The ISBP code requirement differs depending on the transfer type and the corresponding CNAB240 segment (e.g., segment A for TED/DOC transfers).

DMEE format tree: Are you using the standard SAP format tree BR_FEBRABAN_A, or a custom/bank-specific tree? If custom, which bank are you sending the file to?

Where the ISBP should appear: In which specific segment/field position of the payment file does your bank expect the ISBP code?

Where the ISBP is stored: Do you currently have the ISBP code stored anywhere in your system (e.g., in vendor bank master data)? The standard bank key in SAP typically holds the 3-digit bank code — please confirm if you are storing the 8-digit ISBP separately.

Attachment: Could you please resend the attachment you mentioned? It does not appear to have come through.

Your answers will allow us to determine whether this can be addressed through standard DMEE tree configuration or requires an enhancement.

Best regards,
Luciano

entry.title: Re: \nERROR BRAIN_DEV (135): Angef.Datenmenge zu groß.
entry.content: Why not, maybe you have more data than last year, so now the limit is reached. To check the details, you can use RSRT, configure it in the same way as SAC. In the ABAP system you set a break-point in exception class CX_RSROA_TOO_EXPENSIVE in the constructor. In the call stack you probably see class CL_RSR_RRK0_OLAP_EFFORT and method CHECK_SAFETY_BELT, there check field p_fetched_lines.

entry.title: Re: Why Every SAP Analytics Consultant Needs to Learn BDC in 2026 - My Personal Take
entry.content:
Hello @arshiya__safreen 

One comment, regarding your "happy-go-lucky" blog post from the point of view of a SAP BI consultant since 2006 😉

I basically agree, that you should not wait starting to learn... but with implementing there is one saying I've been telling for years now: "With new SAP tools/software, there is no too late, there is only too early" 😮

For example, if you had started with SAP Datawarehouse Cloud early, you could have dropped everything and start all over again with Datasphere ... with functionality that did not exist in the beginning, but only came into place quarter after quarter.

The same in BDC now... in 2025 SAP promised to deliver "lots of" Intelligent Apps... how many do we see so far? 2, 3?

Every customer who started with Datasphere has already implemented all its master data tables & views & dimensions with the capabilities of that time ... there was no Object Store on the Horizon... still today, there is no "complete" master data content as a "SAP managed data product" available  ... of course, the future will bring some more ... even if you think about the proclaimed "Autonomous Enterprise" ... but it will still be a long way ahead.

What I want to say, in my humble opinion: SAP is telling their vision way too far ahead of the real go live date (similar with Knowledge Graph in DSP - known for ~2 years now, seen myself, still not today).

So, should we avoid or neglect new developments like BDC? Definitely not.

Should we try to find the "right time" for implementation (without wasting time and money)? Definitely yes 🙂

What is your opinion on mine?

BR, Martin

entry.title: SAP MDG Cloud Ready Mode: UI Modeling with local annotation files for App F7204A (Manage BP)
entry.content:
How To…

Hide the «Create-Button for Bank Details" in the MDG App “Manage Business Partner – Central Governance  (F7204A)

Date: July 2026, Author: Steffen Ulmer

Goal:

When working with the standard SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) Fiori app F7204A, the Bank Details section is displayed — including a Create button — whenever a user holds the authorization to edit or create a Business Partner record. While this is the expected behavior for standard data maintenance, it can become a compliance concern in governance-driven scenarios.

In MDG governance processes, it is sometimes necessary to restrict end users from creating new (and deleting existing) bank details directly, even if they are otherwise authorized to work with Business Partner records. This guide walks you through a practical, technical approach to hiding the "Create" button in the Bank Details section of app F7204A using local annotation files.

Info

  • Scope note: This guide uses a local annotation file approach, which gives you fine-grained control over UI element visibility and field properties in MDG Fiori apps.
  • Key User Extensibility ("Adapt UI") is not applicable for hiding these buttons in app F7204A. Although Adapt UI is typically the simplest option for UI adjustments, the architectural design of this app prevents button-level hiding through that feature.
  • The technique described here is reusable: the same pattern applies to other action buttons and can also be used to influence data field behavior within the same section.

Before / After Screenshots

Before:

After:

Prerequisites: SAP BTP BAS – UI Adaptation Project & Deployment

SAP MDG on SAP S/4HANA

  • SAP S/4HANA Version PCE (Private Cloud Edition) >=2025

BAS Access

  • Enterprise global account in SAP BTP
  • Subaccount with BAS entitlement configured and subscription created via Service Marketplace
  • Business_Application_Studio_Developer role collection assigned to your user
  • Supported browser: Mozilla Firefox, Google Chrome, or Microsoft Edge

Space & Project Setup

  • Destination to the ABAP system configured in SAP BTP (with HTML5.DynamicDestination and HTML5.Timeout properties)
  • If on-premise: SAP Cloud Connector installed, configured, and connected to the subaccount

Deploy App Variant

  1. User assigned role SAP_UI_FLEX_DEVELOPER (SAP_UI ≥ 7.55) or S_DEVELOP (SAP_UI < 7.55)
  2. Active transport request available in the ABAP system
  3. After deployment: configure a new target mapping in SAP Fiori Launchpad Designer for the new app variant tile

Step by Step

Step 1: Set Up BAS Space

  1. Log on to SAP BTP Business Application Studio.
  2. Use an existing SAP Fiori-type dev space, or create a new one of type SAP Fiori

Step 2: Create the Adaptation Project

  • From the BAS home screen, choose New Project from Template

  • Select SAPUI5 Adaptation Project and click Start

  • Select ABAP as the target environment and click Next.
  • Choose the destination pointing to your SAP S/4HANA system


Note: The system will connect to the backend and read all apps available for adaptation — this may take a moment.

  • From the application list, select: F7204A – Manage Business Partner – Central Governance

  • Complete the wizard by entering:
    • Project Name
    • Application Title
    • Namespace
  • Click Finish to generate the project.

Step 3: Implement the Local Annotation

  • Open the Adaptation Editor: In the Project Explorer, right-click the file manifest.appdescr_variant.
  • Select Open Adaptation Editor.

  • The app opens in the visual editor. Ensure you are on the UI Adaptation tab.
  • Navigate to the Bank Details Section: Switch to Navigation mode within the editor. Search for a Business Partner and navigate into the BP record. Click Edit — the Bank Details section and its Create button will become visible.
  • Add a Local Annotation File: From the Quick Actions panel, choose Add Local Annotation File

  • Select Main Service as the target OData service

  • Save the changes.


The system generates a new annotation XML file under webapp/changes/annotations/ in your project folder.

Step 4: Edit the Annotation XML

  • Switch back to the BAS browser tab and open the generated XML file.

 

  • Insert the following lines at line 16 of the XML file:
<Annotations Target="SAP__self.Container/BusinessPartnerBank">    
<Annotation Term="UI.CreateHidden" Bool="true"/>    
<Annotation Term="UI.DeleteHidden" Bool="true"/>
</Annotations> 

 

These annotations hide the Create and Delete buttons in the Bank Details section of the Business Partner Object Page.

  • Preview the Variant - Open the variant in Preview mode.

 

Verify that the Create and Delete buttons are no longer visible in the Bank Details section.

 

Step 5: Post-Deployment Steps

The following steps are required to make the variant available to end users but are not specific to this use case.

Step

Action

Deploy

Deploy the adaptation project to the target SAP S/4HANA system. Ensure the correct Transport Request and ABAP package are specified.

Catalog Management

After deployment, include the Fiori App Variant in the relevant Fiori catalogs used by the target business roles.

 

Further Reference: For detailed guidance on deploying adaptation projects and managing catalogs, refer to the SAP Fiori Tools – Deploying an Adaptation Project documentation.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Create Kanban Control Cycles with Predefined External Container IDs
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have a question regarding Kanban in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Our customer already has several thousand physical Kanban containers in use. Each container is labeled with a unique external container ID that is physically attached to the container and should continue to be used after the SAP implementation.

Question:

Is there any standard functionality that allows creating Kanban control cycles while assigning predefined external container IDs to the individual Kanban containers?

Ideally, we would like to:

  • Create the Kanban control cycle.
  • Assign existing external container IDs to the Kanban containers during creation or via a mass upload.
  • Avoid SAP generating new container IDs, as the existing physical labels should remain unchanged.

Has anyone implemented a similar scenario, or is there a recommended best practice (e.g. migration object, API, configuration, or enhancement option) to map existing physical Kanban container IDs to SAP Kanban containers?

Any guidance or experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Unable to see the Hana cloud details while sharing the Data product from BDC
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are trying to share a data product from SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) to an SAP HANA Cloud instance. However, the HANA Cloud instance is not visible in BDC during the target selection process.

Our observations:

  • SAP Business Data Cloud tenant: https://vp-bdc-poc16.eu10.hcs.cloud.sap

  • SAP HANA Cloud instance is deployed in the EU10 region.

entry.title: Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise – New Live Session Series
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence is transforming enterprise architecture. Architects are no longer focused solely on designing systems and integrating processes; they are increasingly responsible for orchestrating intelligence, context, and autonomous execution across the enterprise.

To help Enterprise Architects navigate this shift, SAP Learning is hosting the #AutonomousEnterpriseArchitec live session series. This expert-led live session series explores the technologies, architectural principles, governance practices, and data foundations that are shaping the Autonomous Enterprise.

Important: While the sessions form a connected learning journey, each session can be booked and attended independently. Whether you're interested in a single topic or want to follow the complete series, you'll gain practical insights and actionable guidance from every session.

Explore the Sessions:

  • Unpacking the Autonomous Enterprise: The What, Why, and How for Architects  Understand the vision, business value, and architectural foundations driving autonomous enterprises, and explore the five building blocks that make this vision tangible. 🔗 Registration
  • Architecting from Intent to Action: Joule Work and the Knowledge Graph Learn how SAP's AI capabilities connect business intent with intelligent execution through context-rich enterprise data, semantic understanding, and agent orchestration. 🔗 Registration 
  • The Architect's Playbook for Building Your Own SAP Agents Discover practical approaches for designing, building, and deploying SAP AI agents using the SAP Business AI Platform and related services. 🔗 Registration
  • An Architect's Guide to AI Agent Lifecycle Management and the SAP Family of Models Explore governance, monitoring, optimization, and the AI models powering next-generation enterprise applications. Learn how to manage AI agents throughout their lifecycle and establish enterprise-scale governance. 🔗 Registration  
  • The AI North Star Architecture and AI Golden Path: From Reference to Reality Gain insights into SAP's AI-Native North Star Architecture and discover proven architectural patterns that help organizations accelerate AI adoption while maintaining enterprise standards. 🔗 Registration 
  • Data Foundations for an Autonomous Enterprise: From Fragmented Systems to Trusted Context Understand why trusted, connected, and governed data is the critical foundation for AI-driven enterprises and autonomous decision-making. 🔗 Registration 
  • The Role of Enterprise Architects in Driving the Autonomous Enterprise Explore how Enterprise Architects evolve from system designers to transformation orchestrators and governance stewards in an AI-driven future. This closing session also brings together the key learnings from across the series. 🔗 Registration

One Series, Multiple Entry Points

Whether you're an Enterprise Architect, Solution Architect, Technology Leader, or AI enthusiast, this series provides valuable guidance on moving from AI experimentation to scalable enterprise transformation.

Follow the complete journey to build a holistic understanding of the Autonomous Enterprise or register only for the sessions that align with your current priorities.

📌Browse all sessions

entry.title: The Process Consulting Agent Is Generally Available: Get Started Now
entry.content:
The Process Consulting Agent in SAP Signavio is now generally available. In this blog, we’ll explore its key capabilities, the value it delivers, and how you can get started today.

Get to know the Process Consulting Agent

The Process Consulting Agent helps organizations understand, analyze, and optimize their business processes. By combining process mining, intelligent data analysis, and conversational AI, it delivers process insights, identifies bottlenecks and inefficiencies, and recommends next-step actions, all through a simple chat interface.

At its core, the Process Consulting Agent is powered by a multi-agent system that seamlessly orchestrates complex process flows and data, delivering insights and actionable recommendations grounded in thousands of industry best practices.

Ask "Are there inefficiencies in my order to cash process?" , "Where can I improve?" and get a clear answer with context, reasoning, and next-step guidelines.

See it live:

 

Process Mining and Analysis in everyone’s hands

The Process Consulting Agent delivers value across your entire organization. For analysis experts, it accelerates routine tasks; allowing you to dig deeper into strategic questions. For business users without analytics backgrounds, it democratizes process insights, so you can run your own analysis and make faster, data-driven decisions.

Here are examples of how different roles benefit:

Process Owner: You're wondering why invoice processing is taking longer than usual. Instead of manually tracing through dozens of transactions, simply ask the agent. It pinpoints exactly where delays occur and gives you a ranked list of quick fixes to tackle first, saving you hours of investigation.

Business Analyst: You're tasked with finding cost-saving opportunities across multiple departments. Rather than building multiple reports, chat with the agent to discover inefficiencies, and uncover where you could save money. You focus on strategy while the agent handles the heavy lifting.

Operations Manager: You're managing a complex supply chain and want to spot emerging problems before they impact delivery. Leverage the agent to monitor your KPIs in real time, ask the agent to suggest preventive actions based on best practices and improve resiliency.

Finance Leader: You need to understand how your accounts payable process compares to best-in-class performers. The agent provides competitive insights, quantifies potential savings, and helps you track improvement progress, all without requesting custom reports from IT.

 

How to Activate the Process Consulting Agent

You can activate the Process Consulting Agent in just a few steps. With 300 actions* per tenant workspace included every month, you can start your conversational process analysis right away.

Before activating, confirm the following are in place:

  • SAP Signavio Process Insights and Intelligence package
  • SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub license
  • Completed onboarding, including the SAP BTP formation for the SAP Signavio Process Insights tenant

Then follow these steps:

  1. In the navigation panel, go to Workspace Settings → AI Services (under Users and Access).
  2. Accept the SAP AI Terms (one-time step; skip if already accepted).
  3. Activate the Base AI version of the Process Consulting Agent for the desired user group.

As a user, you will receive a notification as you approach the 300 actions* limit.

Once reached, the system automatically blocks additional actions until the first day of the next calendar month or until Premium AI is activated.

If you reach the monthly limit, you can activate the Process Consulting Agent as a Premium AI feature, where additional usage consumes AI units.

For all details, please refer to the help page.

Next Steps

We're excited to see more organizations discover the Process Consulting Agent and experience the next generation of process mining and analysis Questions about the setup? Drop them in the comments below. Already using it? Tell us what question you asked first and the benefits you are experiencing!

 

*Note: An action is defined as a single operation the AI performs while completing a task. One user request can trigger one or more actions.

entry.title: Re: statical delivery date is not changing
entry.content:
Hello @Madhusudanc 

Check the note 2280529 - Statistical delivery date not automatically updated - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA. It explains the logic in details.

In short - if statistical delivery date is the same as delivery date and delivery date is changed, then statistical delivery date is changed too automatically. If statistical delivery date is already different then delivery date, then statistical delivery date is not updated automatically anymore. That is done on purpose to support vendor evaluation, on-time KPI.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Announcing Preview of SAP LogServ integration with Google SecOps for SAP Cloud ERP Private customers
entry.content:
This blog was co-authored by Ajith Urimajalu (Engineering, Google), Brad Nixon (Product, Google), Jesper Christensen (SAP Architect, Google), Krishna Rajapantula (ECS Lead of Security Engineering Operations), @Hemanth_Kusampudi (ECS Service Owner)

We are thrilled to announce the preview integration between SAP LogServ and Google Security Operations, exclusively for SAP Cloud ERP Private customers around the globe.

SAP LogServ is an SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) service aimed to provide SAP Cloud ERP Private customers access to their infrastructure and OS logs. LogServ centralizes the logs from all systems, applications, and ECS services used by a registered customer into a storage location.

Main Features include:

  • Near Real Time Log Collection: Integrate into a customer's SIEMs or Log Management systems.
  • Log Retention: Retain logs indefinitely or with an adjustable retention policy for each data source.
  • Recovery of logs: Ability to recover logs which were retained.

Google SecOps, with its Triage and Investigation Agent (TIN), is setting new standards in cybersecurity innovation. TIN is an AI-powered investigation assistant embedded directly in Google SecOps. The agent automatically analyzes alerts using Mandiant principles and industry best practices, determines if the alerts are true or false positives, and provides a summarized explanation for its assessment. By dynamically refining search queries, enriching data with Google Threat Intelligence (GTI), and reconstructing process trees, it offers a cutting-edge solution to protect critical business data.

The innovative integration with SAP LogServ enables SAP RISE customers to leverage the powerful security analytics and AI-driven threat intelligence capabilities of Google SecOps, seamlessly integrated with the log management features of SAP LogServ. This collaboration aims to enhance your security operations and provide comprehensive protection beyond your SAP environments to your whole IT estate.

LogServ complements the existing Google SecOps threat monitoring and detections with the log types provided by SAP ECS as the system provider. This includes logs like: ICM, SAP Web Dispatcher, SAP Gateway, SAP HANA Audit logs.

Benefits of the Integration

  • SAP-centric Setup: Using SAP LogServ and the Application Telemetry Collector, logs are seamlessly forwarded to Google SecOps via Cloud Storage and Bindplane agents.
  • Enhanced Security: Gain deeper insights into potential threats and vulnerabilities spanning beyond your SAP systems looking at your IT estate using Google SecOps' Unified Data Model (UDM).
  • Centralized Monitoring: Streamline your security operations by adding log data from SAP LogServ directly into your Google SecOps feeds.
  • AI-Powered Response: Using the Triage and Investigation Agent (TIN), significantly reduces manual triage time as the AI evaluates incoming alerts, executes an investigation plan, and provides a structured analysis of its findings and reasoning.
  • Proactive Threat Detection: Respond to threats in real-time utilizing detection rules built specifically for SAP environments

Securing the Crown Jewels: Why SAP RISE Customers Need Google SecOps and SAP LogServ Today

Your SAP environment houses the crown jewels of your enterprise—financial data, supply chain operations, and critical IP. Yet, for many Security Operations Centers (SOC), the SAP landscape remains a blind spot. Traditional security tools often fail to ingest, parse, and analyze complex SAP application logs at scale, leaving organizations vulnerable to sophisticated lateral movement and insider threats.

The days of flying blind are over.

Why This is a Game-Changer for SAP Security

Until now, correlating threats that originate in your broader IT environment and move into your SAP systems was a highly manual, complex, and slow process. By pairing SAP LogServ’s deep, system-level visibility with Google SecOps' planetary-scale analytics and AI, we are eliminating those silos.

For SAP Managers and CISO Leaders:

  • Mandiant Expertise in a Box: Google SecOps now includes the Triage and Investigation Agent (TIN). TIN is an AI-powered investigation assistant that doesn't just flag alerts—it acts on them. Using Mandiant principles and industry best practices, TIN automatically analyzes alerts, determines true/false positives, dynamically refines search queries, and reconstructs process trees. It summarizes its findings instantly, drastically reducing manual triage time and mitigating SOC burnout.
  • Proactive Defense, Not Reactive Scrambling: Instead of waiting for an audit failure or a breach notification, you can now respond to threats in real-time utilizing out-of-the-box detection rules built specifically for SAP environments.

For SAP Technical Architects and Security Engineers:

  • Unprecedented Visibility: SAP LogServ (an SAP Enterprise Cloud Services offering) centralizes critical infrastructure and OS logs—including ICM, SAP Web Dispatcher, SAP Gateway, and SAP HANA Audit logs.
  • Unified Data Model (UDM): Using the Application Telemetry Collector, logs are seamlessly forwarded to Google SecOps via Cloud Storage and Bindplane agents. Once ingested, they are mapped to Google’s UDM, allowing your security engineers to query SAP data alongside network, endpoint, and cloud data in sub-seconds.
  • Near Real-Time Collection & Indefinite Retention: Maintain compliance and investigative power with adjustable, long-term log retention and near real-time SIEM integration.

The Real-World Impact

Consider this scenario: A compromised credential from a phishing attack allows a threat actor to access a standard corporate workstation. From there, they attempt to pivot into your SAP Web Dispatcher.

Without this integration, the initial phishing alert sits in one queue, while the SAP login anomaly sits buried in an un-parsed log file. With SAP LogServ and Google SecOps, TIN instantly correlates the endpoint alert with the SAP HANA audit log, reconstructs the process tree, enriches the data with Google Threat Intelligence (GTI), and presents your SOC analyst with a structured analysis and containment plan—all before the attacker can exfiltrate your financial data.

Don't Wait for the Next Threat. Get Started Today.

Don't miss this opportunity to enhance your security posture with the powerful combination of SAP LogServ and Google SecOps. Threat actors are increasingly targeting ERP environments. Delaying your SAP security modernization is a risk you cannot afford.

Your 3-Step Action Plan:

  1. Engage SAP: Coordinate with your SAP ECS representative immediately to enable LogServ and provision your destination Cloud Storage bucket and Pub/Sub notifications.
  2. Deploy & Ingest: Follow our detailed documentation to deploy the Application Telemetry Collector and create your Google SecOps feeds: [Set up log ingestion for SAP RISE].
  3. Activate Defenses: Download and deploy our out-of-the-box detection capabilities directly from the [SAP Detection Rules repository on GitHub].

Secure your SAP crown jewels with the intelligence of Mandiant and the scale of Google.

To get started, coordinate with your SAP ECS representative to enable LogServ and provision your destination Cloud Storage bucket and Pub/Sub notifications. For detailed instructions on preparing your environment, deploying the Application Telemetry Collector, and creating Google SecOps feeds, visit the Set up log ingestion for SAP RISE documentation.

You can also explore out-of-the-box detection capabilities in Google’s SAP Detection Rules repository on GitHub.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Modify G/L Account During Goods Receipt Pos...
entry.content:
Dear @Devanshu_Mishra108,

Question 1.

Yes. During a PO-based Goods Receipt the account assignment including the G/L account, is inherited from the purchase order and cannot be changed during GR posting. SAP explicitly confirms that goods receipts inherit the PO account assignments and that these cannot be changed

More details below:

SAP Help – Sourcing and Procurement: Goods Receipts and Service Entry Sheets

Question 2.

No. SAP does not provide an SSCUI, business role, authorization or UI adaptation that makes the inherited G/L Account editable during PO-based Goods Receipt

The available extensibility for the Goods Receipt app supports custom fields and validations but it does not allow the PO account assignment to be replaced during GR posting

More details below:

SAP Help – App Extensibility: Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document

Question 3.

The G/L account should be corrected in the purchase order before posting the Goods Receipt, provided that the PO is still changeable. Depending on the workflow configuration the PO change may require renewed approval

Preventive checks or automatic derivation can be implemented during PO processing using the BAdI MM_PUR_S4_PO_MODIFY_ACCOUNT – Modify Purchase Order Account Assignment Data, which supports changing fields such as GLACCOUNT. This option applies at PO level, not during GR posting

More details below:

SAP Help – App Extensibility: Manage Purchase Orders

Question 4.

The standard correction depends on the document status:

  • Before GR, correct the existing PO if the system still allows the account assignment to be changed
  • If the PO cannot be corrected, create a corrected PO item or a new PO
  • If the GR has already been posted, reverse the GR correct the PO and post the GR again
  • The PR only needs to be recreated if required by the company procurement or approval process

Therefore, it is not always necessary to recreate both the PR and PO. However the G/L account cannot be corrected directly during Goods Receipt

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: 🇦🇺 Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (Sydney, Australia)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have some basic knowledge about JavaScript as we are using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant.

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you will become acquainted with the theory behind GenAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you will become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

Prerequisites:

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • We will be using the primary environment as described in the prerequisites here https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-cap-llm/blob/main/prerequisites.md#prerequisites

Date: Friday, September 4, 2026 

Event Address: The Mint, 10 Macquarie Street, Sydney.

Google maps location: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c7UG1Mgn2Yoq6SCd7

The language of the event and the content will be English.

How to register?

Use the Eventbrite registration link to register for the CodeJam. The CodeJam event is available as an add-on to the SAP Inside Track(SIT), Sydney event https://www.eventbrite.com.au/e/sap-inside-track-sydney-2026-tickets-1988688376468

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 20 attendees.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: Connect to sap HANA Database from Microsoft power BI(Business Intelligence)
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing this guide.

For teams using direct HANA access primarily to retrieve S/4HANA business data, an API-based alternative may also be worth considering.

Power BI Connector for SAP connects through activated SAP OData services and does not require direct HANA database access or local HANA ODBC configuration:

https://www.sap.com/products/data-cloud/partners/metrica-software-inc-power-bi-connector-for-sap.html

It lets users select SAP services, entities, fields, and server-side filters, then exposes the result through an OData endpoint for Power BI.

It is not a replacement for HANA SQL, Calculation Views, or DirectQuery, but it can centralize access configuration for supported S/4HANA reporting scenarios.

A 30-day free trial is available.

Documentation:
https://metricasoftware.com/docs/sap/

Cheers,

Anton

entry.title: Re: Sourcing & Procurement in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Hi @ElisabethE , Thanks for trying this and for the detailed observations!

A couple of things to check based on your two symptoms:
  1. Navigation goes to ME21N instead of the Fiori “Manage Purchase Orders”
  • In S/4HANA Cloud, adopting a PR from “Manage Purchase Requisitions – Professional” (F1048A) should launch the Fiori app “Manage Purchase Orders” (F0842/F0842A). If your system opens ME21N instead, it usually means the Fiori target mapping isn’t active for your user or you’re on Private Cloud/On‑Prem where GUI is still configured as the default target.
  • What to check:
    • Verify your business role (e.g., SAP_BR_PURCHASER or equivalent) includes the PO app and its target mappings (F0842/F0842A).
    • In the Fiori Launchpad content manager, confirm the semantic object/action used from F1048A resolves to the Fiori app, not to ME21N.
    • If you’re on Private Cloud/On‑Prem, ensure the Fiori content is activated and assigned; otherwise the system may fall back to ME21N.
  1. PR header attachments (DMS) not appearing on the PO
  • Header‑to‑header attachment transfer from PR to PO is not universally available in all releases. There is a product roadmap item specifically for this capability (“Transfer of header attachments from purchase requisitions to purchase orders”), which indicates it’s planned/enhanced in cloud editions and may depend on your release.
  • Practical checks/workarounds:
    • Test item‑level attachments on the PR and adopt them to the PO to see if they carry over in your release.
    • Verify your DMS configuration and document types are set up for adoption across the PR and PO business objects (and whether you’re using the Fiori Attachment Service vs. classic GOS links).
    • For Private Cloud/On‑Prem, if header transfer isn’t delivered for your release, consider an enhancement (e.g., a BAdI in the PR→PO adoption step) to copy the DMS links from PR header to the new PO.

entry.title: 🇦🇺 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Sydney, Australia)
entry.content:
This SAP Build low-code/no-code SAP CodeJam focuses on giving you hands-on experience for building event-triggered processes with SAP Build Process Automation and how to work with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh to send those events to your processes.

You will build an event-based scenario that lets users create S/4HANA business partners, setting off a process where:

  1. An employee approves the creation of a site access badge.
  2. A second employee, determined based on the details of the business partner, may also need to approve the badge.
  3. The process calls an external, 3rd-party badge system and waits to be called back.
  4. A user creates the badge, and the badge system calls back the process instance.
  5. A notification with the badge ID is sent.

The scenario is built in 2 halves:

  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh: You will get to know all about events, CloudEvents, events in the SAP landscape, the process for publishing and subscribing to events, and your way around SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Then you will create a REST Delivery Point in advanced event mesh to forward events to your SAP Build on your SAP BTP trial account.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: You will create a process from scratch to handle the issuance of site access badges. You will learn the basics of SAP Build Process Automation (projects, triggers, conditions, creating instances, monitoring, and the Inbox), and also use some of the more recent or advanced features (event triggers, form events, waiting for a API callback).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: 
    • Introduction to the CodeJam
    • Prerequisites

Date: Friday, September 4, 2026 

Event Address: The Mint, 10 Macquarie Street, Sydney.

Google maps location:  https://maps.app.goo.gl/c7UG1Mgn2Yoq6SCd7

The language of the event and the content will be English.

How to register?

Use the Eventbrite registration link to register for the CodeJam. The CodeJam event is available as an add-on to the SAP Inside Track(SIT), Sydney event https://www.eventbrite.com.au/e/sap-inside-track-sydney-2026-tickets-1988688376468

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 20 attendees.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Variable Pay worksheet
entry.content:
Hi @Raluca_Barbacariu 

When an employee shows on a worksheet but with blank data it's usually because the employee history record isn't being tied to a bonus plan. You think you did it right but the system says otherwise. You need to troubleshoot it there.

 

Also it probably means that in your XML you have this setting set to "false" (otherwise an employee would never show on the worksheet with a blank row due to their record not being tied to a bonus plan). I recommend setting it to "true" as below:

 

Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Variable pay for Bonus calculation Equation custom formula
entry.content:
@mfpabon23 "since the bonus calculation is prorated based on the employee's hire date and the time spent on LOA." why don't you use employee history eligibility rules to simply not retrieve the time spent on LOA ? The if-then feature in Custom Basis formula is not designed with this in mind (I know because I helped define requirements to engineering teams for this enhancement). It's designed for companies who have different types of employees (for example as part as a merge-acquisition) legally requiring (as per their contract) different basis equations. Not to remove LOA time from the target amount. For this we use employee history eligibility rules.

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/time-off-integration-in-compensation-eligibility-rules/ba-p/13579851

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/understanding-how-to-retrieve-only-the-right-employee-records-in-variable/ba-p/14339797


Also if you have access to provisioning you can look at the detailed logs of why your employee history job is failing: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/how-to-troubleshoot-failing-variable-pay-employee-history-or-bonus/ba-p/13504809

Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Create Kanban Control Cycl...
entry.content:
Dear @Matthias_G_,

No. In Public Cloud predefined external IDs cannot be assigned as the technical SAP Kanban Container IDs

When a Kanban control cycle is created, you define the number of containers and SAP automatically creates the individual container records with system-generated KanbanContainer IDs

The PP-KAB - Kanban Control Cycle migration object and the released Kanban Control Cycle API can be used to create the control cycles, but they do not provide a field for uploading predefined IDs for the individual containers. The documented Public Cloud configuration migration and API options do not provide a way to replace SAP-generated container IDs with predefined external IDs

The recommended approach is therefore to keep the SAP-generated container ID and maintain a separate mapping:

Existing physical container ID -> SAP Kanban Container ID

For several thousand containers I recommend maintaining this mapping through a small custom upload and storing it in a custom extension or mapping table. A container-level custom field may also be considered, subject to verification that it is writable through the API in your current S/4HANA Cloud release

If the existing barcode is only used for visual identification the current labels can remain unchanged. However, if the barcode must be scanned to change the Kanban status the standard process expects the SAP container ID. In that case the practical alternatives are:

  • a small custom scanner application or integration that converts the external ID into the SAP container ID before calling the status-change API

or

  • adding a second barcode containing the SAP-generated container ID

Summary:

The existing physical IDs cannot replace SAP internal Kanban Container IDs through standard migration, configuration or API functionality. The practical Public Cloud solution is to retain the SAP generated IDs and maintain an external-ID mapping, with a small extension where the existing barcodes must continue to be used

More details below:

SAP Help – Create Kanban Control Cycle

SAP Help – Available Migration Objects

SAP Help – Change Kanban Container Status

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Upskill Faster with AI-Powered Assistance: Learn Your Way
entry.content:
Upskill Faster with AI-Powered Assistance: Learn Your Way

Everyone learns differently. Some learners prefer to read, others learn better by listening, while others benefit from seeing complex concepts represented visually.

SAP is now giving learners more choice in how they engage with learning content on learning.sap.com, with the option to use AI-powered assistance through Google NotebookLM.

Using NotebookLM Studio, learners can take content from an SAP Learning Journey and choose the format that works best for them - from a podcast-style Audio Overview or Mind Map, to summaries and study materials that help reinforce their understanding.

One Learning Journey, Different Ways to Learn

Imagine three learners working through the same SAP learning courses.

One prefers listening and generates an Audio Overview to reinforce what they've just studied.

Another is a visual learner and creates a Mind Map showing the relationships between key concepts.

A third prefers a concise study guide, helping them review the key points before returning to particular topics in greater depth.

They are all working towards the same learning objectives and using the same trusted SAP learning content as their foundation - but the way they interact with that content is different.

This is where AI-powered assistance can become particularly valuable. Rather than replacing structured learning, it provides another layer of support, helping learners engage with concepts in the way that works best for them.

The SAP Learning Journey remains at the heart of the experience. AI-powered assistance simply gives learners more ways to interact with that content, understand concepts faster and personalize their learning experience.

Same trusted SAP learning. More ways to learn.
